exemplar.title: To what extent does the principle of individualism help one in achieving the American Dream? An analysis of Tim Burtonâs movie âBig Fishâ.

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the principle of individualism help one in achieving the American Dream? An analysis of Tim Burtonâs movie âBig Fishâ.

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Topic and focus are communicated accurately and effectively, with clear definitions and sustained linkage to the research question.
The research question is stated clearly, focused, and consistently connected to the discussion throughout.
The structure of the introduction and conclusion reinforces coherence between the argument and evidence.
weaknesses: No dedicated methodology section explaining sourceâselection criteria or analytical procedures.
Evidence of informed source selection is uneven, with popular websites cited alongside academic texts without justification.
The conclusion omits discussion of methodological limitations or suggestions for further inquiry.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work excels at communicating its topic and maintaining a sharply focused research question, fully satisfying the topâband descriptors in those strands. However, the lack of an articulated methodology and clear rationale for source selection prevents a maximum mark. On balance, a score of 5/6 reflects the very strong focus and question clarity tempered by an uneven method presentation.

strengths: Primary quotations are accurately timeâstamped and integrated into the analysis.
Secondary references are generally used to support interpretive points on symbolism and cultural context.
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on generalâinterest sources limits scholarly depth and rigor.
Specialized filmâstudies terminology is largely absent, resulting in sporadic and sometimes imprecise conceptual vocabulary.
Some illustrative plot retellings are only loosely tethered to external scholarly support.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source application and subject knowledge are appropriate but not consistently rigorous, and the sporadic use of key terminology diminishes the overall scholarly precision. These weaknesses place the work in the lower half of the 6-point band, meriting a score of 2/6.

strengths: Arguments are coherent and consistently linked back to the research question.
Analytical passages (e.g., fish metaphor, Spectre critique) show thoughtful interpretation and thematic insight.
Conclusion synthesizes key findings and reflects personal engagement with the topic.
weaknesses: Analysis is intermittently interrupted by extended plot summary instead of deeper critical interrogation.
Opposing viewpoints and methodological limitations are only briefly acknowledged, reducing evaluative depth.
Limited engagement with broader film theory restricts the range of scholarly counterarguments.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates an adequate level of critical thinkingâwith clear relevance to the question and some nuanced evaluationsâbut lacks the sustained depth, comparative perspectives, and theoretical sharpness of higher bands. A mid-band score of 4/12 accurately reflects these qualities.

strengths: Includes all major components (title page, contents page, headings) and clear paragraphing.
Table of contents is comprehensive and aids navigation.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing, disrupting full structural completeness.
Footnote markers are sometimes out of sequence and the citation style is inconsistent.
Bibliography entries mix different styles and omit stable identifiers (DOIs).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the overall structure assists reader navigation, the omission of page numbers and inconsistent formatting of footnotes and bibliography place the work in the bottom band for presentation. A score of 1/4 reflects these significant lapses.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionââTo what extent does the principle of individualism help one in achieving the American Dream? An analysis of Tim Burtonâs movie Big Fishââis explicitly stated on the title page and is restated in the introduction.  It is focused on a single film, a single thematic relationship and uses evaluative language (âto what extentâ) that invites sustained debate.  Throughout the essay, paragraph topic sentences consistently re-engage with the question, demonstrating a strong link between the RQ and ensuing discussion.  Consequently, the strand merits the highest band.

Analytical passagesâsuch as the unpacking of the fish metaphor or the critique of Spectre as a stultifying utopiaâdemonstrate thoughtful interpretation and a logical connection to the research question.  However, these insights are interspersed with extended plot summary and assertions that are not always interrogated against alternative readings or counter-evidence.  The analysis therefore shows competence but lacks the sustained depth, comparative perspective and theoretical sharpness characteristic of higher bands.

Quotations from the screenplay are accurately time-stamped and integrated into the prose, and most secondary references are used to support interpretive points (e.g., Adamsâs definition of the American Dream; Buddhist symbolism of goldfish).  However, reliance on general-interest articles limits scholarly depth, and some illustrative plot retellings are only loosely tethered to external support.  Overall, source application is generally appropriate but not consistently rigorous, matching the 3-4 band descriptor.

Layout conventions are only partially met.  The student supplies a title, word count and table of contents, but omits page numbersâa mandatory componentâand uses variable heading formats.  Footnote sequencing issues occasionally disrupt readability.  Consequently, layout shows âsome errors and/or missing elements,â placing it at the lower end of the 1â2 band.

The student demonstrates a sound grasp of Big Fishâs narrative, symbolism and cultural context, correctly invoking concepts such as the âmythicâ American Dream and individualism.  Yet engagement with film-specific theory (mise-en-scÃ¨ne, cinematography, narrative structure) is limited, and some sections lapse into descriptive synopsis rather than analytical exposition.  Knowledge is therefore clear but not consistently deep or systemically framed by scholarly perspectives, situating the work in the middle band.

The essay includes all required components (title page, contents page, headings, bibliography), and paragraphing is clear; however, page numbers are missing and a few footnote markers are out of sequence.  These lapses indicate that while the overall structure assists reader navigation, certain expected elements are absent or inconsistently applied.  Performance therefore matches the 1â2 band for structure appropriateness.

The essay employs a reasonable range of sources (primary film text plus seven secondary items) that are generally pertinent to the research question.  Citations are mostly accurate, and the student shows some discernment in selecting materials on symbolism, the American Dream and cultural context.  Nevertheless, several sources are popular rather than academic, and no evidence is offered that broader scholarship on film theory or individualism was systematically consulted.  Research therefore meets âadequateâ standards with clearâthough not consistentârelevance, meriting a score toward the lower end of the 4â6 band.

The essayâs purposeâto examine how individualism aids attainment of the American Dream through Burtonâs Big Fishâis announced in the title, reiterated in the opening paragraph and sustained through clearly-labelled sections.  Definitions of both âindividualismâ and âAmerican Dreamâ are provided early, and each major body section explicitly links its discussion back to the research focus.  Minor digressions into plot summary occur, but they do not obscure the central line of inquiry.  Overall, the topic is conveyed accurately, coherently and with an appropriate scope for a 4,000-word EE, fully satisfying the top-band descriptor.

The discussion is generally coherent: each section concludes with a link back to the guiding question, and the conclusion synthesises key findings about individualism and the American Dream.  Nonetheless, evaluation of opposing viewpoints or methodological limitations remains brief, and some reasoning rests on unexamined assumptions (e.g., equating personal myth-making with tangible success).  Argumentation is thus âgood with some inconsistencies,â aligning with the 4â6 band but nearer the lower end.

Although the student cites primary (timestamps) and secondary sources and explains footnote conventions, there is no dedicated methodology section that clarifies how sources were selected, why particular theoretical lenses were adopted, or what criteria governed the filmic analysis.  Evidence of informed selection is only partial: several popular websites (GradeSaver, Vanity Fair, 540WestMain blog) sit beside a handful of academic texts without justification of reliability.  These weaknesses place the work in the middle band, where methodology is âmostly completeâ but unevenly articulated.

Key terms such as âindividualism,â âAmerican Dream,â and occasional references to symbolism are used accurately; however, specialised film-studies terminology (e.g., diegesis, mise-en-scÃ¨ne, cinematography) is largely absent.  Conceptual vocabulary appears sporadically and without systematic application, resulting in explanations that often rely on general literary language rather than discipline-specific precision.  This aligns with the 1â2 band, where terminology is present but limited and sometimes imprecise.

annotations.comment: Page numbers are missing from the footer of every page, which impedes navigation and does not meet the formatting requirement.
Citation style for the Adams quote varies from later references; establish and apply a consistent in-text citation format to enhance scholarly rigor.
There is no dedicated methodology section explaining source selection criteria or analytic procedures, making the research approach unevenly articulated.
Heading format varies between pages; ensure consistent use of font size and style for section headers to maintain a polished layout.
The term âAmerican Dreamâ is often repeated without direct reference back to its academic definition; reconnect to the conceptual framework for precision.
Listing fantastical elements is engaging, but the discussion lacks analysis of how cinematography or sound design conveys the dual nature of myth and reality.
The narrative oscillation is noted but without a clear methodological outline for how these juxtapositions were analyzed in the film-text.
This paragraph resumes plot summary instead of synthesizing thematic insights; focus on connecting scenes directly to your argument about individualism.
The essay would benefit from integrating additional film theory sources to strengthen interpretive claims about narrative structure.
The title is precise and directly states the research focus, immediately signaling a clear and sustained line of inquiry into individualism and the American Dream as examined through Big Fish.
The student has provided a word count, satisfying the formatting requirement and demonstrating awareness of the EE guidelines.
The introduction is highly anecdotal and personal, but it lacks methodological framing explaining how the research will be conducted or which analytical lenses are adopted.
The historical definition of the American Dream from Adams is well integrated with an appropriate citation, giving strong context and demonstrating subject understanding.
The footnote format disrupts reading flow; ensure footnote markers are sequential and formatted consistently to improve readability.
The analysis asserts the fish symbolizes the American Dream but does not consider alternative interpretations or counter-examples; extend evaluation for depth.
The discussion of the fish metaphor would be strengthened by applying film-studies terminology (e.g., mise-en-scÃ¨ne, diegesis) to show deeper subject mastery.
The conclusion effectively ties back to the research question, reinforcing coherence between the argument about individualism and the evidence from the film.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly lists all major sections with corresponding page numbers, aiding reader navigation.
Page numbers are omitted from the footer here as well, which should be added to meet the required formatting conventions.
The final discussion is rich but partly repetitive; condense overlapping ideas to create a sharper, more impactful closing statement.
The essayâs ending underscores a personal and philosophical resonance with the American Dream, showing strong engagement and reflection on the research topic.
The bibliography entries mix citation styles (MLA, APA); apply a single, consistent style guide to all references for professional presentation.
URLs are provided without DOIs or stable identifiers; include permanent links or DOIs where possible to improve academic credibility.
The final entry lacks consistent formatting for title and publisher; ensure each source follows the chosen citation style (italicization, punctuation, date order).
This paragraph relies heavily on plot summary rather than developing critical analysis; consider reducing narrative recap to focus on thematic interpretation.
Repeating the fish metaphor emphasis shows a strong engagement with the filmâs recurring motif and reinforces the thematic focus.
No methodological rationale is provided for selecting specific film scenes or secondary sources, limiting transparency of the research approach.
This section reverts to descriptive synopsis instead of interrogating how Bloomâs identity transformations support the research question.
Analysis of Bloom as an archetype of the American Dream is asserted broadly but would benefit from engaging with scholarly counterarguments.
The depiction of Spectre as a utopian trap demonstrates nuanced evaluation of conformity versus individualism.
Interpretations of the Spectre sequence would be strengthened by referencing visual evidence (framing, lighting) to show how Burton conveys its false idyll.
The mention of Buddhist goldfish symbolism requires a more precise scholarly source citation; verify and reference an academic authority for credibility.
The conclusion restates findings but omits discussion of methodological limitations or suggestions for further inquiry, limiting critical self-evaluation.
